content:
I'm implementing per-file VM ioctl support and need to store Arc<Vm<'bound>> in a DriverFile implementor, but DriverFile requires Self to be a concrete type with no lifetime parameter. 'bound comes from IoMem<'bound> holding a real &'bound Device<Bound> .

Has anyone dealt with storing lifetime-bound objects in a DriverFile implementor?
@Deborah Brouwer @Daniel Almeida
Sorry what is this DriverFile thing?

DriverFile is the trait DRM drivers implement for their file-private data. It requires Self to be a concrete type with no lifetime parameter. My vm_create ioctl creates Vm<'bound> from device registration data and needs to store Arc<Vm<'bound>> in the DriverFile implementor.
I think you need to wrap in a Devres to erase the lifetime.

Just to confirm the approach: would it make sense to use IoMem::into_devres() to get Devres<IoMem<'static>>, then drop the 'bound lifetime parameter from Mmu and the rest of the chain (AddressSpaceManager, Vm, etc.)?

The 'bound only comes from IoMem holding &'bound Device<Bound>, so wrapping it in Devres would make Vm lifetime-free and DriverFile could stay as-is.
Is it possible to have the Devres wrap the Arc<Vm<'bound>> value? cc @Danilo Krummrich
Avoid having to store Arc<Vm<'bound>> inside DriverFile is the better approach I'd say
You can store it inside registration data and just store a handle in DriverFile
@Gary Guo the file is the per-session context and we'd ideally like to use it to manage the lifetime of the session objects, like this VM

I tried adding a lifetime parameter to DriverFile, but ForeignOwnable erases types to void* across the FFI boundary, requiring T: 'static for soundness. Vm<'a> can't satisfy this, so XArray<Arc<Vm<'a>>> doesn't compile. Removing the 'static bound would work, but the compiler-enforced safety would degrade to unsafe caller responsibility -- I'm not sure if that's acceptable.
There's nothing unsound for removing T: 'static
You just need to add where bounds to a few places, see Box's impl

Thanks! I'll try removing T: 'static, following Box's impl. There's a second issue: with a lifetime on DriverFile, I need reg_data and file_data to share the same lifetime, but accessing them through nested for<'a> closures produces two independent lifetimes. Is there a recommended pattern for this?

Alice Ryhl said:

Is it possible to have the Devres wrap the Arc<Vm<'bound>> value? cc Danilo Krummrich

DevresLt can do that, but only if Vm<'a> does not borrow something whose lifetime strictly ends at 'a. IOW, 'a must only originate from owned device resource values.
Eventually we want that the file private data to carry a lifetime and only live until the file is closed or the DRM device is unregistered (whichever happens first).
I just started looking into this a few days ago (in parallel to some related TTM topics).
Are you planning to work on this yourself, or would you like me to go ahead and put together a prototype? Just want to make sure we're not duplicating effort.
@Danilo Krummrich
@Alvin Sun I already got some code, which I think I could send out this week. In case something else comes up, would you be interested in taking over?

Danilo Krummrich said:

Alvin Sun I already got some code, which I think I could send out this week. In case something else comes up, would you be interested in taking over?

Sounds good, looking forward to the patches! If you get tied up, I'd be happy to take over with your guidance.
